title: ranunculus fertilis
intro: Ranunculus fertilis, also known as the lesser celandine or pilewort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus fertilis include its ability to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus fertilis typically involve drying the plant's rhizome and roots, which can then be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
